2604.06906.title: The AI Skills Shift: Mapping Skill Obsolescence, Emergence, and Transition Pathways in the LLM Era
2604.06906.paper_summary: As Large Language Models reshape the global labor market, policymakers and workers need empirical data on which occupational skills may be most susceptible to automation. We present the Skill Automation Feasibility Index (SAFI), benchmarking four frontier LLMs -- LLaMA 3.3 70B, Mistral Large, Qwen 2.5 72B, and Gemini 2.5 Flash -- across 263 text-based tasks spanning all 35 skills in the U.S. Department of Labor's O*NET taxonomy (1,052 total model calls, 0% failure rate). Cross-referencing with real-world AI adoption data from the Anthropic Economic Index (756 occupations, 17,998 tasks), we propose an AI Impact Matrix -- an interpretive framework that positions skills along four quadrants: High Displacement Risk, Upskilling Required, AI-Augmented, and Lower Displacement Risk. Key findings: (1) Mathematics (SAFI: 73.2) and Programming (71.8) receive the highest automation feasibility scores; Active Listening (42.2) and Reading Comprehension (45.5) receive the lowest; (2) a "capability-demand inversion" where skills most demanded in AI-exposed jobs are those LLMs perform least well at in our benchmark; (3) 78.7% of observed AI interactions are augmentation, not automation; (4) all four models converge to similar skill profiles (3.6-point spread), suggesting that text-based automation feasibility may be more skill-dependent than model-dependent. SAFI measures LLM performance on text-based representations of skills, not full occupational execution. All data, code, and model responses are open-sourced.
2604.06906.paper_summary_zh: 隨著大型語言模型重塑全球勞動市場，政策制定者和工人需要有關哪些職業技能可能最容易受到自動化影響的實證數據。我們提出了技能自動化可行性指數（SAFI），對四個前沿大型語言模型——LLaMA 3.3 70B、Mistral Large、Qwen 2.5 72B 和 Gemini 2.5 Flash——在涵蓋美國勞工部 O*NET 分類法中所有 35 種技能的 263 項基於文本的任務中進行基準測試（總共 1,052 次模型調用，失敗率 0%）。通過與來自 Anthropic Economic Index 的現實世界 AI 採用數據（756 種職業，17,998 項任務）進行交叉參考，我們提出了一個 AI 影響矩陣——這是一個詮釋框架，將技能定位於四個象限：高取代風險、需要提升技能、AI 增強和較低取代風險。主要發現：（1）數學（SAFI：73.2）和編程（71.8）獲得了最高的自動化可行性分數；主動聆聽（42.2）和閱讀理解（45.5）獲得了最低分；（2）一種“能力需求反轉”，即在 AI 曝露的工作中需求最強烈的技能是我們基準測試中大型語言模型表現最差的技能；（3）觀察到的 AI 互動中有 78.7% 是增強，而非自動化；（4）所有四個模型的技能輪廓趨於相似（3.6 分差距），這表明基於文本的自動化可行性可能更依賴於技能而不是模型。SAFI 測量大型語言模型在技能的基於文本的表現，而非完整的職業執行。所有數據、代碼和模型回應均為開源。
2604.06906.author: Rudra Jadhav et.al.
